perturbation.original_text: During the term of and subject to this Agreement, Contractor shall perform the Manufacturing Outsourcing Services including purchase, assemble, manufacture, configure, test and deliver to NICE' freight forwarder in Contractor's facility, under the terms set forth below, and NICE shall purchase from Contractor, and Contractor shall sell to NICE, such quantities of units of the Products according to NICE' Purchase Orders, from time-to-time as detailed below, at the quoted prices set forth in Appendix C.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of and subject to this Agreement, the Service Provider shall perform the Manufacturing Outsourcing Services including purchase, assemble, manufacture, configure, test and deliver to NICE' freight forwarder in the Service Provider's facility, under the terms set forth below, and NICE shall purchase from the Service Provider, and the Service Provider shall sell to NICE, such quantities of units of the Products according to NICE' Purchase Orders, from time-to-time as detailed below, at the quoted prices set forth in Appendix C.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Contractor' is replaced with 'Service Provider'. While they might seem synonymous, without explicitly stating so, using different terms for the same entity introduces ambiguity. Throughout the contract, the entity is referred to as 'Contractor', but now in this section, it is 'Service Provider'. It creates confusion if the entities are the same, creating uncertainty in enforcement of the contract as a whole.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Contractor's obligations to execute the Manufacturing Outsourcing Services pursuant to this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date, subject to the following provisions:
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Contractor' to 'Service Provider' in the modified text directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which specifically refers to 'Contractor'. This creates ambiguity and inconsistency as to which entity is responsible for fulfilling the obligations outlined in the agreement, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as described in the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition. The contract consistently uses 'Contractor', and introducing 'Service Provider' without clarification leads to uncertainty about which entity is bound by the agreement's terms.

perturbation.original_text: NICE shall give Contractor a written forecast of the Products, by type, quantity and expected Due Date (the "FORECAST") as follows: A Forecast shall be a rolling forecast for the current Quarter and the two (2) consecutive Quarters, detailed by week. Upon the Effective Date, NICE shall deliver a Forecast for the period commencing on the expected completion of the Relocation Phase for the first Product, for the current Quarter and for the next coming two Quarters and so on thereafter during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: NICE shall give Vendor a written forecast of the Products, by type, quantity and expected Due Date (the "FORECAST") as follows: A Forecast shall be a rolling forecast for the current Quarter and the two (2) consecutive Quarters, detailed by week. Upon the Effective Date, NICE shall deliver a Forecast for the period commencing on the expected completion of the Relocation Phase for the first Product, for the current Quarter and for the next coming two Quarters and so on thereafter during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Contractor' is replaced with 'Vendor' when recieving forecasts from NICE. Introducing 'Vendor' without clearly defining it as the same entity as 'Contractor' creates uncertainty, particularly around the obligations tied to the 'Contractor' throughout the agreement, such as in section 3.2 where it states: 'Contractor undertakes that in the event of increase in Product requirements VIS-A-VIS the Forecast (i.e. issuance of PO's exceeding'. It becomes unclear whether the 'Vendor' receiving forecasts is subject to the same obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Contractor undertakes that in the event of increase in Product requirements VIS-A-VIS the Forecast (i.e. issuance of PO's exceeding 15 the relevant Forecast), Contractor's manufacturing capacity can be increased, at any time, by thirty percent (30%) beyond the then current Forecast. In addition, in as much as purchase of components and purchase of sub assemblies are concerned, NICE may order Products, which are not included in the Forecast, and Contractor will make its best reasonable commercial efforts to accommodate such order in accordance with NICE' request. It is clarified that in any event Contractor will accept all PO's exceeding the Forecast, subject to updating the Due Date based upon the Total Lead Time for obtaining the required components, which are not available in Contractor's inventory. The Due Date for such PO's will be determined according to the time of obtaining the components. For components/Products the prices for which are not previously agreed under APPENDIX C, the price will be agreed in writing prior to acceptance of the PO.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Contractor' to 'Vendor' introduces a potential ambiguity regarding the party responsible for fulfilling the obligations outlined in the contradicted text, which specifically refers to 'Contractor'. This ambiguity creates a direct conflict, as it's no longer clear whether the 'Vendor' receiving forecasts is also bound by the manufacturing capacity increase requirements stated for the 'Contractor', as highlighted in the explanation. The contradicted text uses 'Contractor' when outlining Product requirements (i.e. issuance of PO's exceeding 15 the relevant Forecast), and if the changed text now specifies 'Vendor' is to receive the forecast, it becomes unclear if those purchase orders still apply to the same entity as the 'Contractor', satisfying Misaligned Terminology definition of in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Either party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement upon the other party seeking an order for relief under the bankruptcy laws of the State of Israel or similar laws of any other jurisdiction, a composition with or assignment for the benefit of creditors, or dissolution or liquidation. NICE shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement upon the merger or acquisition of all or substantially all the business or assets of Contractor (except if within the Flextronix's group and provided Contractor remains an Israeli entity).
perturbation.changed_text: Either party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement upon the other party seeking an order for relief under the bankruptcy laws of the State of Israel or similar laws of any other jurisdiction, a composition with or assignment for the benefit of creditors, or dissolution or liquidation. NICE shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement upon the merger or acquisition of all or substantially all the business or assets of Supplier (except if within the Flextronix's group and provided Contractor remains an Israeli entity).
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Contractor' is replaced with 'Supplier' in the context of merger or acquisition. Since the definition of 'Contractor' has not been modified overall, and it is not explicitly stated that 'Supplier' and 'Contractor' are the same, this creates ambiguity. It is unclear whether a merger or acquisition involving the 'Supplier' triggers the termination clause if 'Supplier' is not explicitly defined as the 'Contractor'. This makes the termination clause relating to business changes uncertain in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EFFECT OF TERMINATION. 16.6.1. Upon notice of termination of this Agreement for any reason whatsoever, the parties shall execute rapidly and efficiently the procedure for termination of the Manufacturing Outsourcing Services by Contractor and the transfer of production to NICE as set forth hereunder and agreed between the parties, provided that Contractor will not be required to incur additional costs for the purpose of assisting NICE in the case of termination. During the notice period, Contractor will continue performance of the Manufacturing Outsourcing Services regarding all PO's received according to the Forecast and will, simultaneously, assist NICE and cooperate with it in the transfer of the Manufacturing Outsourcing Services to NICE or anyone designated by NICE and NICE will continue in performing all its obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Contractor' with 'Supplier' in the termination clause related to merger or acquisition. This creates a contradiction because the 'Contradicted Text' refers to 'Contractor' when specifying obligations upon termination. If 'Supplier' and 'Contractor' are not explicitly the same, it becomes unclear whether the termination procedure outlined in the contradicted text applies to a merger or acquisition of the 'Supplier', thus creating an ambiguity and making the terms of the contract contradictory. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
